agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers. Core workflow: create shipment → mark in transit → mark delivered, with strict state machine enforcement and weight validation.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, governed by a strict state machine and weight constraints."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where a parcel is collected. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where a parcel is to be delivered. Required at shipment creation."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The lifecycle stage of a shipment. Valid states: Pending, InTransit, Delivered. Transitions are strictly forward-only."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial state of a newly created shipment. The shipment exists but has not yet begun moving."},{"term":"InTransit","definition":"The shipment is actively being transported. Cannot revert to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal state. The shipment has reached its delivery address. No further state changes are permitted."},{"term":"Weight","definition":"The physical mass of the parcel in kilograms. Must be positive and at most 1000 kg."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A pricing classification derived from shipment weight: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], Freight (>50 kg up to 1000 kg]."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without origin and destination is logically meaningless — it violates the fundamental definition of a shipment. Data integrity would be corrupt.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment with a null or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Shipment state transitions are strictly forward-only: Pending → InTransit → Delivered. No backward transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing backward transitions (e.g., InTransit → Pending) would corrupt operational state integrity — dispatch, driver assignment, and tracking all depend on monotonic state progression.","testable_assertion":"An InTransit shipment cannot transition to Pending. A Delivered shipment cannot transition to any other state."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Delivered is a terminal state. Once a shipment is Delivered, no state mutation is allowed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivered is a physical reality — the parcel has been handed over. Reversing this would corrupt audit trails, billing records, and customer notifications. This is a subset of BR-2 but called out for emphasis.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly greater than zero.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would corrupt weight-based pricing calculations.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected at creation and at any weight update."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 1000 kg cap is a business policy reflecting current fleet/equipment limits. A VP could raise this to 2000 kg if the company acquires larger vehicles. It is not a physical law.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Weight bracket is derived from weight: Standard for (0, 10] kg, Heavy for (10, 50] kg, Freight for (50, 1000] kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket thresholds and names are pricing policy decisions that could change with a business decision (e.g., adding a 'Super Freight' tier at 500 kg).","testable_assertion":"A 10 kg shipment is Standard. A 10.01 kg shipment is Heavy. A 50 kg shipment is Heavy. A 50.01 kg shipment is Freight."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A newly created shipment starts in the Pending state.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Skipping Pending would mean a shipment is InTransit or Delivered at creation, which corrupts the state machine and bypasses pickup operations.","testable_assertion":"After successful creation, shipment state must be Pending."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A request to create a shipment with a valid pickup address, delivery address, and weight of 5 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The shipment is persisted in Pending state with weight bracket Standard"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A request to create a shipment missing a pickup address","when":"Creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating pickup address is required"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A request to create a shipment missing a delivery address","when":"Creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating delivery address is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"It is transitioned to InTransit","then":"The state changes to InTransit successfully"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment in InTransit state","when":"An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains InTransit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment in Delivered state","when":"Any state change is attempted (to Pending or InTransit)","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment creation request with weight 0 kg","when":"Creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A shipment creation request with weight -5 kg","when":"Creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A shipment creation request with weight 1001 kg","when":"Creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request indicating weight exceeds maximum of 1000 kg"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 10 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Standard"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment with weight 10.01 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Heavy"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 50 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Heavy"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A shipment with weight 50.01 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Freight"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"An attempt is made to transition directly to Delivered","then":"The system rejects the transition — shipments must pass through InTransit first"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight exactly at bracket boundaries (10 kg, 50 kg)","expected_behavior":"10 kg → Standard, 50 kg → Heavy. Boundary values belong to the lower bracket (inclusive upper bound)."},{"scenario":"Attempting Pending → Delivered directly (skipping InTransit)","expected_behavior":"Rejected. The spec implies a linear state machine: Pending → InTransit → Delivered. Skipping states violates the business process — a parcel cannot be delivered without being in transit."},{"scenario":"Weight of exactly 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted. 1000 kg is the maximum, and the rule says 'cannot exceed' — so 1000 is valid and classified as Freight."},{"scenario":"Very small positive weight (e.g., 0.001 kg)","expected_behavior":"Accepted. The rule requires positive weight with no minimum floor beyond zero. Bracket is Standard."},{"scenario":"Shipment with identical pickup and delivery address","expected_behavior":"Not addressed in spec. Likely should be allowed (e.g., internal warehouse transfers) but flagged as an ambiguity."},{"scenario":"Updating weight after shipment is InTransit or Delivered","expected_behavior":"Not addressed in spec. Domain intuition: weight should be immutable after InTransit to preserve pricing integrity, but this is not explicitly stated."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket classification directly determines pricing. Incorrect bracket assignment means revenue leakage (undercharging) or customer disputes (overcharging). The 1000 kg cap bounds liability and fleet utilization.","operational":"Strict forward-only state machine ensures dispatch, tracking, and proof-of-delivery workflows remain coherent. Allowing backward transitions would create phantom shipments that confuse warehouse and driver operations.","user":"Customers expect shipment state to progress monotonically. A shipment reverting from InTransit to Pending would erode trust. Clear weight brackets enable transparent, predictable pricing.","risk":"Primary risk is bracket boundary miscalculation causing systematic pricing errors at scale. Secondary risk is state machine bypass allowing data corruption in audit and billing systems.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Shippers / Customers","impact":"Depend on correct weight bracket for predictable pricing and on accurate shipment state for tracking visibility."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Depend on state machine integrity to coordinate pickups, route assignments, and delivery confirmations."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Weight brackets drive invoicing. Incorrect classification causes revenue discrepancies and reconciliation overhead."},{"group":"Drivers / Couriers","impact":"State transitions trigger their workflow — InTransit means they have custody. Backward transitions would create confusion about parcel responsibility."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is clear on core state machine rules, weight constraints, and bracket definitions. Confidence is not higher because several real-world concerns are unaddressed: whether Pending→Delivered is allowed, whether weight is mutable after creation, what constitutes a valid address, and whether shipments can be cancelled. These are common logistics requirements that a production system would need.","high_confidence_areas":["State machine transitions (forward-only, Delivered is terminal)","Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg","Weight bracket boundaries and classification","Both addresses required at creation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Pending → Delivered skip is allowed (inferred as no, but not explicitly stated)","Whether weight can be updated after shipment creation","Address validation rules (format, completeness)","Cancellation flow — not mentioned at all","Whether shipment can have additional states (e.g., Failed, Returned)"],"assumptions":["Pending → Delivered is not a valid transition; shipments must pass through InTransit","Weight bracket boundaries are inclusive on the upper bound (10 kg is Standard, not Heavy)","Weight is set at creation time and the spec does not address mutability","The 0 boundary for weight is exclusive (strictly greater than zero, not >= 0)","No cancellation or exception states exist beyond what is described"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not explicitly state whether a Pending shipment can skip InTransit and go directly to Delivered. The phrase 'Once in transit, it cannot be sent back to pending' only addresses the InTransit→Pending case. Pending→Delivered is a separate transition not mentioned.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Drivers","Billing"],"options":["A) Allow Pending→Delivered (supports edge cases like in-store pickup)","B) Require Pending→InTransit→Delivered strictly (enforces full logistics workflow)"],"recommendation":"B — Require the full state progression. In logistics, skipping InTransit means no proof of pickup, no driver custody chain, and no tracking data. If in-store pickup is needed, model it as a different flow."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Weight bracket boundary ownership at exactly 10 kg and 50 kg is ambiguous. The spec says '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy, over 50 kg is freight.' Both Standard and Heavy claim 10 kg; both Heavy and Freight could claim 50 kg.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance","Customers"],"options":["A) Boundaries belong to lower bracket: 10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy","B) Boundaries belong to upper bracket: 10 kg = Heavy, 50 kg = Freight"],"recommendation":"A — '0-10 kg is standard' reads as inclusive of 10. 'Over 50 kg is freight' explicitly excludes 50. This gives: Standard (0,10], Heavy (10,50], Freight (50,1000]."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No cancellation or exception handling is described. Real logistics systems require the ability to cancel shipments (at least from Pending), handle failed deliveries, and manage returns. The absence of these flows is a significant gap.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Operations","Finance"],"options":["A) Add a Cancelled state reachable from Pending only","B) Add Cancelled reachable from Pending and InTransit","C) Defer — ship without cancellation for now"],"recommendation":"B — Customers and operations need cancellation from both Pending and InTransit. But this requires PM clarification before implementation."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
